growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (6% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat

mfa.note: MFA-calculated at mu=0.10; measured Yco2/S=2.41±0.09, error 0.5%
relative net flux at mu=0.10, 6% NaCl

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.method: reported (D=mu at chemostat steady state); td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.103=6.73 h
growth_records.snippet: qo2 and qco2, as well as qp and qs, increased with D, reaching 6.80 and 6.74 mmol/g DCW/h, 372.05 umol/g DCW/h, and 3.04 mmol/g DCW/h at D=0.35 h-1 ... Table 1: D=0.103, qs 1.12, qo2 2.61, qco2 2.70, qp 71.12, Yx/s 0.51
growth_records.notes: Direct chemostat steady-state values from Table 1, 6% NaCl, D=0.103 h-1 (mu=D). qs (glucose uptake)=1.12 mmol/gDCW/h; qo2=2.61; qco2=2.70; qp(ectoine)=71.12 umol/gDCW/h. td=ln2/mu=6.73 h. Ectoine is intracellular (not secreted). Temperature: seed stated 37C; fermenter T not explicitly restated in text so assumed same as seed (37C). MFA relative fluxes from Fig 3 text for mu=0.10.

growth_records.notes: Table 1, 6% NaCl, D=0.351 h-1 (highest dilution, residual glucose rising as approaching washout; batch mu_max=0.41 h-1). qs=3.04, qo2=6.74, qco2=6.80 mmol/gDCW/h; qp=372.05 umol/gDCW/h. td=1.97 h.

growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (13% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat

up.method: Herbert-Pirt fit qs=mu/YG+m=0.10/0.897+0.056=0.1675 g/gDCW/h /180.16*1000=0.93 mmol/gDCW/h (YG,m from Table 2)

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.snippet: For the 13% salt... Cx and Cp reaching maxima of 15.89 and 3.03 g/L at D=0.30, 1.29 and 1.67x higher than at D=0.10... Yp/s 0.11 and pectoine 1.03 mmol/g at D=0.10... qco2 decreased by 10.4% (mu=0.10) under 13% vs 6%
growth_records.notes: 13% NaCl, D=mu=0.10 h-1. Per-condition qs/qo2/qco2/qp values are in Fig S5/Table S2 (not numeric in main text); values here computed: qs and qo2 from Herbert-Pirt/Monod fits (Table 2: YG=0.897, m=0.056; Yx/o2=143.472 g/mol, mo2=1.488) => model-fit (is_experimental=false). qco2 derived from stated 10.4% reduction vs measured 6% value 2.70 => 2.42 (experimental basis). qp from fitted qp=1.504mu-0.0633. Cx=15.89/1.29=12.32; Cp=3.03/1.67=1.81; Yco2/S=1.83/(1-0.304)=2.63. Yp/s (0.11) and pectoine (1.03) stated directly. td=6.93 h.

growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (13% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat
growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.snippet: 13% salt: Yp/s 0.11 and 0.13 mmol/mmol, pectoine 1.03 and 1.05 mmol/g at D=0.10 and 0.20... qco2 decreased by 46.7% at mu=0.20 under 13% vs 6%; Yco2/S 41.5% lower at mu=0.20
growth_records.notes: 13% NaCl, D=mu=0.20 h-1. qs/qo2 are Herbert-Pirt/Monod model fits (Table 2 params) => is_experimental false. qco2=5.17*(1-0.467)=2.76 from stated 46.7% reduction of measured 6% qco2. qp from fitted eqn. Yp/s (0.13) and pectoine (1.05) stated directly. Per-D biomass/Cs at 13% mu=0.20 only in Fig S5/Table S2. td=3.47 h.

growth_records.medium: HE9 synthetic medium (13% NaCl), glucose-limited chemostat

up.method: Herbert-Pirt fit qs=0.30/0.897+0.056=0.3905 g/gDCW/h /180.16*1000=2.17 mmol/gDCW/h (Table 2); note qs lower than 6% (2.59) as stated

sec.method: qp=D*Cp/Cx=0.30*3.03/15.89=0.0572 g/gDCW/h /142.15*1000=402 umol/gDCW/h; fitted eqn gives 388

growth_records.cite: Junxiong Yu et al. Decoding metabolic trade-offs in Halomonas elongata: Chemostat-based flux remodeling for industrial ectoine biosynthesis. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2025.07.004. [PMC12318276]
growth_records.snippet: 13% salt, D=0.30: Cx 15.89 g/L, Cp 3.03 g/L, Yp/s 0.20 mmol/mmol, pectoine 1.34 mmol/g, Yco2/s 1.83 mmol/mmol. ED proportion 61%; NADPH 94.4, NADH 352.9 mmol/100mmol glucose
growth_records.notes: 13% NaCl, D=mu=0.30 h-1 - highest-yield condition, directly stated: Cx=15.89, Cp=3.03 g/L, Yp/s=0.20 mmol/mmol, pectoine=1.34 mmol/g, Yco2/S=1.83. qp computed as D*Cp/Cx=0.30*3.03/15.89/142.15*1000=402 umol/gDCW/h (experimental basis; fitted eqn 388). qco2=6.41*(1-0.412)=3.77 from stated 41.2% reduction. qs/qo2 Herbert-Pirt/Monod fits (Table 2). MFA % changes vs 6% and NADPH/NADH production fluxes from Fig 4 text. td=2.31 h.
